document.masterDocTitle: A study on the genus Anaches Pascoe, 1865 (Coleoptera, Cerambycidae, Lamiinae Pteropliini), with a new species and two new synonyms

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: A study on the genus Anaches Pascoe, 1865 (Coleoptera, Cerambycidae, Lamiinae Pteropliini), with a new species and two new synonyms

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Engineering Research Center for Forest and Grassland Disaster Prevention and Reduction, Mianyang Normal University, 166 Mianxing West Road, Mianyang, Sichuan 621000, China. linmeiying 2021 @ 126. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0001 - 9219 - 724 X Am Schlossgarten 6, 07381 Wernburg, Germany

heading.emphasis:
Anaches m-signatus
Lin & Weigel sp. nov.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 1–3.
Anaches m-signatus
sp. nov. ; 1. Holotype, ♂, from Guangxi; 2. Paratype, ♂, from Hunan; 3, Paratype, ♀, from Hunan; a. dorsal view; b. lateral view; c. ventral view. Scale bar = 4 mm.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Diagnosis. This new species belongs to the “narrow banded” species group. It is mostly similar to
A . yitingi Holzschuh & Lin, 2013
, but can be easily distinguished from it by the following features: the whitish band wider; the anterior margins of the whitish bands “V”-shaped, instead of “U”-shaped; the posterior margin of the whitish band forms “m”-shaped, instead of an oblique line; the apex of tergite VIII truncated, not slightly emarginated; the apex of ventral plate of median lobe not as shaped as that of
A . yitingi
.
Etymology. This species is named after the m-shaped macula margin ( Fig. 3a , in the red rectangle) on each elytron.

Distribution. China :
Zhejiang Province , Hunan Province , Guangxi Province .

materialsCitation.collectionCode: IZCAS, IOZ, T, CCCC, DZAE, CAW

materialsCitation.collectionCode: IZCAS, IOZ, T, CCCC, DZAE, CAW
IZCAS, IOZ, T, CCCC, DZAE, CAW
IZCAS, IOZ, T, CCCC, DZAE, CAW
IZCAS, IOZ, T, CCCC, DZAE, CAW
IZCAS, IOZ, T, CCCC, DZAE, CAW

subSubSection.paragraph:
Remarks. This new species is quite similar to
Desisa pterolophioides Gressitt
from Fujian , China ( Gressitt, 1942a ) (now in the genus
Macropraonetha Breuning, 1961
) by color and pubescence pattern, but can be separated from it by the elytron with one tubercle with tufted hairs before basal one fifth, the elytral apex truncated instead of narrow rounded, and the anterior and posterior margins of white band on elytra with different shapes.
